message:
feat(artifacts): implement multi-format Research Brief export

Add comprehensive export/import capabilities for Research Briefs:
- exportToMarkdown() - Human-readable markdown using existing template
- exportToJSON() - Programmatic format with checksum verification
- exportToPDF() - Print-friendly HTML with @media print styles
- importResearchBrief() - Import with integrity validation
- downloadResearchBrief() - Browser download utility
- printResearchBrief() - Direct print dialog trigger

Includes 26 tests covering all export formats, round-trip integrity,
checksum validation, and browser download mocking.

Closes brenner_bot-nu8g.2

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add font imports to layout.tsx (next/font) 2. Define tokens in globals.css 3. Create utility classes or Tailwind config # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Fonts load correctly - [ ] Typography scales smoothly across viewport sizes - [ ] Headlines tight, body text comfortable - [ ] Code/mono text uses JetBrains Mono","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:13:08.992044195-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:35:45.282026352-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:35:45.282026352-05:00","close_reason":"Fluid typography scale added: 8 size tokens using clamp(), line heights, letter spacing, build verified","labels":["design-system","typography","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-84o","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-7yz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:13:46.297570969-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-84o","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1qx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:59.419182619-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-8b10","title":"Task: Implement Program Storage Layer","description":"# Task: Implement Program Storage Layer ## Objective Create file-based storage for research programs with session management. ## Implementation Details ### File Location `apps/web/src/lib/program-storage.ts` ### Storage Structure ``` .research/ └── programs/ ├── programs.json # Index of all programs └── RP-20251230-cell-fate/ # Per-program directory ├── program.json # Program metadata └── sessions.json # Session order and metadata ``` ### Index File Format ```json { "programs": [ { "id": "RP-20251230-cell-fate", "name": "Cell Fate Determination", "status": "active", "sessionCount": 4, "lastActivityAt": "2025-12-31T15:30:00Z" } ] } ``` ### Program File Format ```json { "id": "RP-20251230-cell-fate", "name": "Cell Fate Determination", "description": "Investigating mechanisms of cell fate decisions...", "researchQuestion": "What determines whether a progenitor cell becomes...", "sessions": ["RS-20251230", "RS-20251231", "RS-20260101", "RS-20260102"], "status": "active", "createdAt": "2025-12-30T09:00:00Z", "updatedAt": "2025-12-31T15:30:00Z", "tags": ["epigenetics", "cell-biology", "development"] } ``` ### Core Functions ```typescript // CRUD operations export async function createProgram( input: Omit ): Promise export async function loadProgram(programId: string): Promise export async function updateProgram( programId: string, updates: Partial ): Promise export async function deleteProgram(programId: string): Promise // Session management export async function addSessionToProgram( programId: string, sessionId: string ): Promise export async function removeSessionFromProgram( programId: string, sessionId: string ): Promise export async function reorderProgramSessions( programId: string, sessionIds: string[] ): Promise // Query operations export async function listPrograms( filter?: { status?: ProgramStatus; tag?: string } ): Promise export async function getActivePrograms(): Promise export async function getProgramForSession( sessionId: string ): Promise ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Programs stored in per-program directories - [ ] Index enables fast listing - [ ] Sessions can be added/removed from programs - [ ] Session order is preserved - [ ] Query by status and tags - [ ] Lookup program by session ID ## Dependencies - Task: Define Research Program Schema ## Effort Estimate ~180 lines of code, ~2 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:00.537943629-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T03:06:17.822786817-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T03:06:17.822786817-05:00","close_reason":"Storage layer exists at apps/web/src/lib/storage/program-storage.ts","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8b10","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-2qyl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:33.164614498-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8b10","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-4buj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:51:33.356683016-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8b10","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:42:19.541683209-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-8dy3","title":"Feature: Unit Test Coverage (Real Implementations Only)","description":"# Goal Add comprehensive unit tests for ALL untested modules using real implementations. # Philosophy: No Mocks Tests must use actual implementations: - Real parsers, not mock return values - Real DOM rendering via happy-dom - Real state management, not mock stores - Real event handlers, not spy callbacks **ONLY EXCEPTION**: framer-motion mock (already in test-setup.tsx) because animations cause test timing issues. # Modules Requiring Tests ## Priority 1 - Core Library (apps/web/src/lib/) - `corpus.ts` (8.2KB) - Corpus configuration and data - `prompt-builder.ts` (7.8KB) - Prompt composition logic - `prompts.ts` (2.2KB) - Prompt templates - `search/engine.ts` - Search engine core - `search/hooks.ts` - Search React hooks - `operators.ts` + `operators.local.ts` - Operator definitions ## Priority 2 - Viewer Components (apps/web/src/components/) - `DistillationViewer.tsx` (33KB) - **CRITICAL**: Main document display - `TranscriptViewer.tsx` - Transcript rendering - `QuoteBankViewer.tsx` - Quote bank display - `MetapromptViewer.tsx` - Metaprompt rendering ## Priority 3 - Interactive Components - `SpotlightSearch.tsx` (28KB) - **CRITICAL**: Main search UI - `OperatorSelector.tsx` (10KB) - Brenner operator selection - `SessionForm.tsx` (15KB) - Session creation form - `SessionActions.tsx` (8.7KB) - Session controls - `command-palette.tsx` (14KB) - Keyboard command interface ## Priority 4 - Navigation & Layout - `nav.tsx` (17KB) - Main navigation - `collapsible.tsx` (10KB) - Collapsible sections - `bottom-sheet.tsx` (5.8KB) - Mobile bottom sheet - `comparison-view.tsx` (6.4KB) - Side-by-side comparison - `reading-progress-widget.tsx` (6.0KB) - Reading progress indicator ## Priority 5 - Animation & Utilities - `animated-element.tsx` (6.1KB) - Animation wrapper - `animated-counter.tsx` (2.0KB) - Counter animation - `jargon-text.tsx` (7.8KB) - Jargon inline rendering # Test Patterns to Follow ```typescript // GOOD: Real rendering render(); await userEvent.type(screen.getByRole('searchbox'), 'brenner'); expect(screen.getByText(/§103/)).toBeInTheDocument(); // BAD: Mocked children vi.mock('./SearchResults', () => ({ default: () =>
mock
})); ``` # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Every module has a `.test.ts(x)` file - [ ] Tests use real data fixtures (not mock objects) - [ ] Coverage per module >= 80% lines - [ ] All tests run without network calls (use real local data) - [ ] Test file size roughly proportional to module complexity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:49:44.37797704-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T12:05:57.531171382-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T12:05:57.531171382-05:00","close_reason":"All 5 child tasks completed: Core Library Unit Tests (0meb), Viewer Component Tests (ponv), Interactive Component Tests (ph4p), Animation & Utility Tests (it7r), Navigation & Layout Tests (x712). 80%+ coverage achieved across all modules.","labels":["testing unit-tests no-mocks"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-8dy3","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-vmsb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:36:00.86542915-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} @@ -569,7 +569,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-nof","title":"Task: E2E test infrastructure for detailed logging","description":"Create E2E test infrastructure for detailed, actionable logging. ## Requirements 1. Custom Playwright reporter for detailed output 2. Structured logging (JSON + human-readable) 3. Automatic screenshot on failure 4. Step-by-step execution logging 5. Network request logging 6. Performance timing collection ## Implementation - Custom reporter class - Helper functions for logging - Screenshot utilities - Network interception setup - Timing collection utilities ## Output Format Each test run should produce: - JSON log file with structured data - Human-readable summary - Screenshots folder - Video folder (optional) - Network HAR file (optional)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:11:46.868236393-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:50:14.274378364-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:50:14.274378364-05:00","close_reason":"Added network-logging.ts with network request logging and performance timing collection. Integrated into test fixtures. Custom reporter, structured logging, and step-by-step execution already existed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nof","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xfs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:12.616710566-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nof","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-e6z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:12.755974053-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-nu8g","title":"EPIC: Artifact Generation - Research Briefs & Exports","description":"# Artifact Generation - Research Briefs & Exports ## Purpose Transform ephemeral sessions into durable, shareable research artifacts that capture the intellectual work done. ## Why Artifacts Matter The value of the Brenner Loop isn't just the thinking—it's the **record of the thinking**. Good artifacts: 1. Survive beyond the session 2. Can be shared with collaborators 3. Serve as institutional memory 4. Document the intellectual journey, not just conclusions ## Core Artifact Types ### 1. Research Brief The primary output. A structured document containing: - Executive Summary (2-3 sentences) - Hypothesis Statement (current version) - Hypothesis Evolution (how it changed and why) - Discriminative Tests Identified (ranked by power) - Agent Analysis (consensus and conflicts) - Evidence Summary (if any tests completed) - Brenner Principles Applied (which quotes, which operators) - Recommended Next Steps Format: Markdown with structured metadata header. ### 2. Hypothesis Card (Export) Standalone export of a hypothesis with: - Full card structure - Version history - Robustness score - Confounds identified Format: JSON (for import/programmatic use) or Markdown (for reading). ### 3. Evidence Ledger Export Complete audit trail of: - All tests recorded - Confidence updates over time - Sources and observations Format: CSV (for analysis) or Markdown (for review). ### 4. Session Transcript Full record of the session including: - All operator applications - All agent responses - All user decisions - Timeline of events Format: Markdown with timestamps. ## Structured Output Schema ### Research Brief Metadata ```yaml --- type: research_brief version: 1.0 session_id: SM-2024-001 hypothesis_id: hyp-xyz created_at: 2024-03-15T10:30:00Z confidence: 37% status: under_pressure operators_applied: - level_split - exclusion_test agents_consulted: - devils_advocate - experiment_designer - brenner_channeler tests_identified: 5 tests_completed: 2 brenner_citations: - §42 - §89 - §73 --- ``` ### Export Formats - **PDF**: For sharing with non-technical audiences - **Markdown**: For git storage, collaboration - **JSON**: For programmatic use, import/export - **HTML**: For web viewing with interactivity ## Citation Management Research Briefs should properly cite: 1. Brenner transcript sections (§42, §89, etc.) 2. External sources added to evidence ledger 3. Agent responses (which agent, which session) ## Templates Users may want different Brief styles: - **Executive Summary**: 1-page version for stakeholders - **Technical Detail**: Full version with all data - **Presentation Mode**: Slides-friendly structure - **Academic Format**: Citation-ready for papers ## Dependencies - All other components (aggregates their outputs) ## Success Criteria - Research Briefs are genuinely useful for sharing - Exports work across formats without data loss - Citations are complete and verifiable - Users actually export and share artifacts ","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:24:43.824490832-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:52:04.484964534-05:00","labels":["artifacts","brenner-loop"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-qtsb","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:24:43.826755175-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-an1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:24:59.187965005-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-vw6p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:24:59.220548574-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:24:59.251620488-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-njjo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:24:59.28090674-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.1","title":"Design Research Brief Template","description":"# Design Research Brief Template ## Purpose Create the template and structure for the primary output artifact: the Research Brief. ## Research Brief Sections ### 1. Metadata Header ```yaml --- type: research_brief version: 1.0 session_id: SM-2024-001 hypothesis_id: hyp-xyz created_at: 2024-03-15T10:30:00Z final_confidence: 37% status: under_pressure --- ``` ### 2. Executive Summary 2-3 sentences capturing: - The hypothesis investigated - Key finding (supported/challenged/unclear) - Primary recommendation ### 3. Hypothesis Statement Current formulation with: - Statement - Mechanism - Domain ### 4. Hypothesis Evolution If hypothesis changed during session: - Original → Current transformation - Trigger for each change - Visual evolution diagram (optional) ### 5. Discriminative Structure - Predictions if true - Predictions if false - Falsification conditions ### 6. Operators Applied For each operator used: - What was discovered - Key outputs ### 7. Agent Analysis - Devil's Advocate findings - Experiment Designer protocols - Brenner Channeler wisdom - Consensus and conflicts ### 8. Evidence Summary If evidence recorded: - Tests run - Results - Confidence trajectory ### 9. Brenner Principles Which transcript sections were relevant: - §42: Level Split - §89: Exclusion Test - etc. ### 10. Recommended Next Steps Prioritized actions: 1. High priority: [action] 2. Medium priority: [action] 3. etc. ## Template Engine Use a template system that can: - Fill from session data - Handle missing sections gracefully - Export to multiple formats ## File Location `apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/artifacts/research-brief-template.ts` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All sections defined - [ ] Template handles missing data - [ ] Metadata schema complete - [ ] Template readable and useful ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:32:10.994542734-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:14:12.212300524-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:14:12.212300524-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","labels":["artifacts","brenner-loop","research-brief"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:32:11.02616862-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-an1n.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:35:33.401656581-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-njjo.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:35:33.447995536-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.2","title":"Implement Multi-Format Export (Markdown, PDF, JSON)","description":"# Implement Multi-Format Export (Markdown, PDF, JSON) ## Purpose Allow users to export Research Briefs in multiple formats for different use cases. ## Export Formats ### Markdown (.md) - Primary format - Git-friendly - Human-readable - Can convert to other formats ```typescript function exportToMarkdown(brief: ResearchBrief): string; ``` ### PDF (.pdf) - For sharing with non-technical audiences - Print-friendly layout - Professional appearance Options: - Browser print-to-PDF (simplest) - Library like jsPDF or react-pdf - Server-side generation with puppeteer (future) ```typescript async function exportToPDF(brief: ResearchBrief): Promise; ``` ### JSON (.json) - For programmatic use - Import/export between sessions - API responses ```typescript function exportToJSON(brief: ResearchBrief): string; ``` ### HTML (.html) - Self-contained web view - Email-friendly - Interactive elements preserved (optional) ```typescript function exportToHTML(brief: ResearchBrief): string; ``` ## UI for Export ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Export Research Brief │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Format: │ │ ○ Markdown (.md) - Recommended │ │ ○ PDF (.pdf) │ │ ○ JSON (.json) │ │ ○ HTML (.html) │ │ │ │ Options: │ │ □ Include full agent responses │ │ □ Include evidence details │ │ □ Include evolution graph │ │ │ │ [Cancel] [Export] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ## File Location `apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/artifacts/export.ts` `apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/artifacts/ExportModal.tsx` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Markdown export works perfectly - [ ] PDF export produces readable document - [ ] JSON export round-trips correctly - [ ] UI provides clear options ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:32:11.284762432-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T02:32:11.284762432-05:00","labels":["artifacts","brenner-loop","export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:32:11.286680102-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:35:23.140449099-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.2","title":"Implement Multi-Format Export (Markdown, PDF, JSON)","description":"# Implement Multi-Format Export (Markdown, PDF, JSON) ## Purpose Allow users to export Research Briefs in multiple formats for different use cases. ## Export Formats ### Markdown (.md) - Primary format - Git-friendly - Human-readable - Can convert to other formats ```typescript function exportToMarkdown(brief: ResearchBrief): string; ``` ### PDF (.pdf) - For sharing with non-technical audiences - Print-friendly layout - Professional appearance Options: - Browser print-to-PDF (simplest) - Library like jsPDF or react-pdf - Server-side generation with puppeteer (future) ```typescript async function exportToPDF(brief: ResearchBrief): Promise; ``` ### JSON (.json) - For programmatic use - Import/export between sessions - API responses ```typescript function exportToJSON(brief: ResearchBrief): string; ``` ### HTML (.html) - Self-contained web view - Email-friendly - Interactive elements preserved (optional) ```typescript function exportToHTML(brief: ResearchBrief): string; ``` ## UI for Export ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Export Research Brief │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Format: │ │ ○ Markdown (.md) - Recommended │ │ ○ PDF (.pdf) │ │ ○ JSON (.json) │ │ ○ HTML (.html) │ │ │ │ Options: │ │ □ Include full agent responses │ │ □ Include evidence details │ │ □ Include evolution graph │ │ │ │ [Cancel] [Export] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ## File Location `apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/artifacts/export.ts` `apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/artifacts/ExportModal.tsx` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Markdown export works perfectly - [ ] PDF export produces readable document - [ ] JSON export round-trips correctly - [ ] UI provides clear options ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:32:11.284762432-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T19:43:04.512855361-05:00","labels":["artifacts","brenner-loop","export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:32:11.286680102-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:35:23.140449099-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.3","title":"Build Citation Manager for Brenner References","description":"# Build Citation Manager for Brenner References ## Purpose Properly cite Brenner transcript sections and external sources throughout artifacts. ## Citation Types ### 1. (2023). "Social Media and Teen Mental Health." Journal... - Instagram Algorithm Change Dataset (2021).
